title: Intensely Smoky Brazilian Chicken Tagine
description: A jammy and flaky Brazilian dish featuring clear honey and char-grill flavors, cooked in a traditional clay tajine vessel, this intensely smoky chicken tagine is perfect for a midnight craving and is low in carbs.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, whisk together smoked paprika, garlic powder, salt, black pepper, and char-grill seasoning.
Add the chicken pieces to the bowl and toss to coat evenly with the spice mixture.
Heat the olive oil in the clay tajine vessel over medium heat on the stovetop.
Add the chicken to the tajine vessel and cook until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Add the clear honey to the tajine vessel and stir to combine with the chicken and spices.
Cover the tajine vessel with a lid and transfer to the preheated oven.
Bake for 30 minutes, then reduce the oven temperature to 350 degrees Fahrenheit and continue baking for an additional 20 minutes.
Remove the tajine vessel from the oven and let it cool for a few minutes before serving.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
